mnemonics.title: Page Replacement Algorithms
mnemonics.content: FIFO: FIRST IN FIRST OUT - LIKE A QUEUE AT A STORE. LRU: LEAST RECENTLY USED - LIKE CHECKING WHICH BOOK YOU READ LONGEST AGO. OPTIMAL: PERFECT FUTURE KNOWLEDGE - LIKE KNOWING EXAM QUESTIONS IN ADVANCE.

mnemonics.title: Memory Fragmentation Types
mnemonics.content: INTERNAL: INSIDE THE BOX - SPACE WASTED WITHIN YOUR ALLOCATED BLOCK. EXTERNAL: OUTSIDE SCATTERED - FREE MEMORY SCATTERED EVERYWHERE LIKE CHANGE IN A POCKET.

mnemonics.title: Thrashing Signs
mnemonics.content: HIGH PAGE FAULT RATE + LOW CPU UTILIZATION = THRASHING. SYSTEM SPENDS MORE TIME SWAPPING THAN EXECUTING.

acronyms.usage: Hardware component that translates virtual addresses to physical addresses and provides memory protection.
Hardware cache that stores recent virtual-to-physical address translations for fast lookup.
Page replacement algorithm that evicts the oldest page in memory.
Page replacement algorithm that evicts the page least recently accessed.
Address used by programs; must be translated to physical address by MMU.
Actual address in RAM where data is stored.
Contains process information including memory management details like page tables.

visualTips.visualization: Think of a library card catalog (virtual) vs actual books on shelves (physical). You request a book, and the librarian (OS) brings it if not on the reading table (RAM).
TLB is like a personal dictionary of frequently looked-up words, much faster than searching through a full encyclopedia (page table).
Imagine a student constantly running between library (disk) and desk (RAM) to get books - no time actually reading (executing).

analogies.analogy: Think of a novel printed on sheets of paper (pages) stored in a filing cabinet (memory). You don't need all pages at once, just retrieve requested sheets.
A large restaurant menu divided into sections: Appetizers, Main Course, Desserts - each is a logical segment that can be referenced by name.
Like a student's open books and notes on a desk while studying - only what you're actively using, not the whole bookshelf.
Moving furniture between storage unit (disk) and your apartment (RAM) - useful when you have more stuff than space in your apartment.
